purpose: Extends the beyond-PLI analysis to five years: every incentive-scheme launch in India's Cabinet-decision register 2021-2025 (plus the 2026 YTD register already analyzed), classified by sector and instrument type, with outlays and PRIDs. Built by five parallel classification agents over the repo's PIB index (data/pib_index.sqlite, 83,734 releases 2020-2026). Tests whether 2026's pivot away from production-linked design is a trend or a blip.
method: Five parallel year-backfills of the PIB day-wise register (one request per day, all ministries via the day page's ministry grouping) merged into the index; five classification agents each swept their year's 'Cabinet approves' rows (~178-257 per year), deduplicated multi-PRID postings, excluded routine decisions (road/rail sanctions, MSP/DA fixations, plain continuations), and fetched detail pages for outlays. 2026 rows come from the beyond_pli_incentives bulletin.
headline: The pivot away from production-linked design is a five-year trend, not a 2026 blip: NEW production-linked scheme launches fell 11 (2021) to 1 (2022) to 1 (2023) to 0 (2024) to 2 (2025, both hybrids) -- and even at the 2021 peak, production-linked outlay was only 11.2% of newly committed money. The 'linked' payout base itself migrated: production-linked (2021) to design-linked to EMPLOYMENT-linked (ELI 2025, Rs 99,446cr -- larger than any single PLI scheme).

yearly_summary.era: The PLI big bang -- plus the quiet mission money
yearly_summary.signature: 11 PLI schemes launched (telecom, IT-hardware, pharma, food, solar, white goods, ACC, steel, textiles, auto, drones) + Semicon India Rs 76,000cr -- but RDSS Rs 3.04L cr, AMRUT 2.0 Rs 2.77L cr and SBM-U 2.0 Rs 1.42L cr carried 6x the money
BSNL revival Rs 1.64L cr, ECLGS +Rs 50,000cr, agri interest subvention Rs 34,856cr; only solar PLI Tranche-II was new PLI; Biofuels amendment pulled E20 forward to 2025-26
Green Hydrogen Mission Rs 19,744cr (SIGHT = PLI-like), BESS VGF, PM-eBus Sewa VGF Rs 20,000cr central, PM Vishwakarma, Quantum Mission; IT-hardware PLI 2.0 the lone PLI

yearly_summary.era: The capex-subsidy year -- zero new PLI
yearly_summary.signature: Four ISM semiconductor units approved (Rs 1.29L cr investment), PM-Surya Ghar Rs 75,021cr rooftop capex subsidy, offshore wind VGF, PM E-DRIVE Rs 10,900cr demand-side, NMEO-Oilseeds, 12 industrial cities, space VC fund, BioE3

yearly_summary.era: Funds & mega-packages; the payout base migrates to jobs
yearly_summary.signature: RDI fund Rs 1L cr, ELI Rs 99,446cr (employment-linked!), shipbuilding package Rs 69,725cr, Critical Mineral Mission Rs 34,300cr, Export Promotion Mission Rs 25,060cr + CGSE Rs 20,000cr; ECMS and REPM the two (hybrid) production-linked entries

Per the beyond-PLI bulletin: Urban Challenge Fund Rs 1L cr, gasification VGF Rs 37,500cr, BHAVYA Rs 33,660cr parks, BMI insurance pool, NIPU-2026, ECLGS 5.0 -- land/credit/risk instruments; MPMS (PLI-LSEM successor) the only output-linked entry

cross_cutting_findings: Production-linked was never where the money was: even in 2021, the 11 PLI launches (Rs 1.17L cr) were 11.2% of the year's Rs 10.5L cr of classified new commitments -- infra missions (RDSS, AMRUT 2.0, SBM-U 2.0) carried 6x the outlay with none of the narrative.

The 'linked' design migrated from output to inputs to jobs: production-linked (2021) -> design-linked (DLI, semicon) -> employment-linked (ELI 2025, Rs 99,446cr, larger than any single PLI) -> 2026's pure de-risking instruments.

Coal gasification shows the escalation pattern for a repo-flagged gap: Rs 8,500cr incentive (Jan 2024, PRID 1999219) -> Rs 37,500cr scheme (May 2026, PRID 2260621) -- a 4.4x escalation on the same import-substitution target within 28 months.

A critical-minerals chain assembled in under a year (2025): National Critical Mineral Mission Rs 34,300cr (Jan) + recycling incentive Rs 1,500cr (Sep) + rare-earth magnets Rs 7,280cr (Nov) -- the new import-substitution frontier, upstream of every PLI sector's supply chain.

Maritime became a package sector without ever getting a PLI: flagging subsidy Rs 1,624cr (2021) -> Vadinar ship repair (2026) -> Rs 69,725cr shipbuilding package + Rs 12,980cr BMI insurance pool -- consistent with the trade analysis' freight/insurance exposure findings.

Ethanol's instrument trail is visible end-to-end in the register: Biofuels amendment advancing E20 (2022, PRID 1826265) -> procurement mechanism (2025, PRID 2097305) -- the precedent the abstract's Act 3 documents, running on offtake+pricing, never a PLI.

Caveat: counts and outlays are Cabinet gate-1 commitments; multi-year outlays are not annualized and mission totals mix central/state/market shares. Grand totals are indicative, not additive fiscal cost.
